steps.title: Find the key requirement
steps.content: The passage requires selecting a maple tree native to North America with a maximum height of under 60 feet. The decisive detail is a specific combination of both traits — not height alone or origin alone.

steps.title: Match the data to the criteria
steps.content:
Red maple is native to North America and has a maximum height of 60 feet, satisfying both requirements.

The rejected choices fail for specific reasons: silver maple reaches a maximum of 70 feet (exceeds the 60-foot limit); sugar maple reaches a maximum of 75 feet (exceeds the 60-foot limit); Norway maple is not native to North America.

That matches Choice C — red maple..
